name: Thirst
description: A hydration stat decays over play and must be restored through water sources, drinks, or environmental access.
design_notes: Thirst is most effective when it shapes route planning around safe water, containers, purification, and temperature exposure. Designers need clear decay rules, contaminated water behavior, recovery transactions, and UI warning thresholds so players can distinguish manageable dehydration from sudden punishment.
design_purpose: Make water sources, containers, purification, and shelter choices matter.
Create exploration pressure that differs from hunger because hydration often has shorter recovery windows.
Connect hot environments, exertion, disease, and inventory planning into one readable survival need.
player_fantasy: The player feels vulnerable in harsh environments and relieved when they secure clean water.

parameters.name: hydration_stat_id
parameters.description: Stat identifier used by decay, recovery, warnings, and dehydration penalties.

parameters.name: thirst_decay_per_minute
parameters.description: Baseline hydration drain while the survival clock is active.

parameters.name: water_recovery_source_id
parameters.description: Water item or source that restores hydration through a validated use transaction.

parameters.name: contamination_rule_id
parameters.description: Rule that determines whether drinking also applies disease, poison, or filtration requirements.

parameters.name: dehydration_effect_id
parameters.description: Penalty applied after hydration crosses its depletion threshold.

parameters.name: hydration_warning_threshold_id
parameters.description: Warning band table used by UI, audio cues, and tutorial prompts.

edge_cases: A player drinks from a source while inventory capacity changes the container or empty bottle state.
Rain, swimming, or shelter changes hydration context but should not automatically refill the stat.
Hot temperature zones multiply thirst decay while a pause, sleep, or fast-travel state is active.
A water source is depleted or contaminated between client preview and server confirmation.
common_bugs: Drinking consumes a container but fails to apply hydration because source validation happens after item mutation.
Contamination effects apply even when the purification recipe or filter item was used successfully.
Thirst warnings spam every frame near the same threshold instead of using threshold crossing events.
Offline decay empties hydration repeatedly after each login because last_decay_tick is not updated.
balancing_notes: Thirst usually feels more urgent than hunger; tune warnings early enough for players to seek water before penalties start.
Purification and containers add meaningful planning only when safe and unsafe sources are visually distinct.
If dehydration blocks sprinting, ensure players are not trapped far from recovery sources without a fallback.
accessibility_notes: Use text labels, icons, and optional audio warnings so hydration state is not conveyed only by screen effects.
Allow reduced dehydration penalties or slower decay for players who find constant upkeep stressful.

implementation_notes.unity: Track hydration in a survival stat component with decay modifiers from temperature, activity, and status effects. Drinking should reserve the water item_instance_id, validate contamination_rule_id, mutate container state, apply recovery, and replicate the result if multiplayer is enabled.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep water sources as data-bearing Area or interactable nodes that call a central hydration service. The service should own decay ticks, warning band transitions, and save fields for current hydration, last decay tick, and active dehydration_effect_id.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use a replicated survival component for hydration and server-authoritative drink requests. Validate water source id, container item, contamination tags, and player access before applying recovery and updating UMG warning state.
implementation_notes.web: Represent water sources and containers as simulation records rather than UI-only buttons. Persist hydration points, source depletion, and last authority tick so browser reloads or offline progress do not duplicate decay.

example_games.title: The Long Dark
example_games.note: Uses hydration alongside hunger, temperature, and fatigue survival pressures.

example_games.title: Subnautica
example_games.note: Uses water supply as part of long exploration planning.
